parsed.clarification_question: Does the review request focus primarily on content aspects like logic, evidence, novelty, and citations (R1.2, R1.3, R1.4, R1.7) or on presentation aspects like structure and writing polish (R1.5, R1.6)?

rationale.R1.2: Reviewing the section centrally involves checking the logical consistency of the pipeline description and evaluation claims.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation expects alignment verification between reported results and claims in the artifact.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing novelty against prior works mentioned is a key part of reviewing this methods and results section.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization at the section level is centrally relevant for improving the narrative arc in the review.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence-level grammar and flow is a central responsibility in reviewing the writing quality.
rationale.R1.7: Verifying citation accuracy and literature coverage is expected in a review of this research section.
rationale.RX.1: The review should centrally address whether uncertainties in the framework and evaluation are properly disclosed.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims in sensitivity and robustness statements is a central cross-cutting concern in the projection.
rationale.RX.3: Ensuring the description adheres to the stated scope of the framework and dataset is centrally important.
rationale.RX.4: Identifying potential downstream harms in the medical AI context is a key responsibility in reviewing the section.
rationale.RX.5: Checking provenance of datasets and models like LIDC-IDRI and LUNA16 is centrally expected in the review.
